title: Quiz: VQE Applications for Chemistry and Materials
description: Test your understanding of VQE for quantum chemistry, including molecular Hamiltonians, ansatz selection, active space methods, and benchmark molecules

questions.question: In the second quantization form of the molecular Hamiltonian, what do the one-body integrals h_pq represent?

options.text: Electron-electron repulsion energies
Kinetic energy and nuclear attraction for a single electron
Nuclear-nuclear repulsion energies
Spin-orbit coupling interactions

questions.explanation: The one-body integrals h_pq = <p|T + V_nuc|q> capture the kinetic energy of electrons and their attraction to the nuclei. These terms involve only single electron operators and describe the motion of individual electrons in the field of the nuclei.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of the Jordan-Wigner transformation in VQE?

options.text: To reduce the number of qubits required
To map fermionic operators to qubit operators while preserving anti-commutation relations
To optimize the variational parameters
To compute molecular integrals classically

questions.explanation: The Jordan-Wigner transformation maps fermionic creation and annihilation operators to qubit operators (Pauli strings). It uses strings of Z operators to track the fermionic parity, ensuring that the anti-commutation relations of fermions are preserved in the qubit representation.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using a UCCSD ansatz over a hardware-efficient ansatz for chemistry problems?

options.text: UCCSD always has shallower circuit depth
UCCSD is physically motivated and systematically captures electron correlation
UCCSD requires fewer parameters
UCCSD is immune to barren plateaus

questions.explanation: UCCSD (Unitary Coupled Cluster Singles and Doubles) is derived from classical coupled cluster theory and includes single and double excitation operators that systematically capture electron correlation. This chemical intuition often leads to faster convergence and more accurate results than generic hardware-efficient ansatze.

questions.question: What is the purpose of active space selection in VQE calculations?

options.text: To increase the accuracy of the calculation
To reduce computational cost by freezing core electrons and truncating virtual orbitals
To eliminate the need for error mitigation
To simplify the Jordan-Wigner transformation

questions.explanation: Active space selection reduces the number of qubits and circuit depth by focusing on the most chemically relevant orbitals (typically those near the Fermi level). Core electrons are frozen and high-energy virtual orbitals are excluded, making the calculation feasible on NISQ devices while retaining chemical accuracy.

questions.question: In VQE, the variational principle guarantees that the computed energy is always an upper bound to the true ground state energy.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: True. The variational principle states that E_VQE = <psi(theta)|H|psi(theta)> >= E_0 for any trial wavefunction. The computed energy is always greater than or equal to the true ground state energy, providing a rigorous upper bound.

questions.question: For a minimal basis H2 molecule, how many spin orbitals (and thus qubits) are required?

options.text: 2 qubits (2 spatial orbitals)
4 qubits (2 spatial orbitals x 2 spin states)
8 qubits (4 spatial orbitals x 2 spin states)
1 qubit (1 molecular orbital)

questions.explanation: H2 in a minimal (STO-3G) basis has 2 spatial orbitals (one 1s orbital on each hydrogen). Each spatial orbital accommodates 2 spin states (alpha and beta), giving 4 spin orbitals. Under Jordan-Wigner mapping, each spin orbital maps to one qubit, requiring 4 qubits total.

questions.question: Which of the following are valid strategies for selecting an active space? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Select orbitals closest to the HOMO-LUMO gap
Always include all electrons in the active space
Freeze core electrons that don't participate in bonding
Exclude very high-energy virtual orbitals

questions.explanation: Effective active space selection involves: (a) selecting orbitals near the Fermi level (HOMO-LUMO gap) which are most important for chemistry, (c) freezing core electrons that don't change during reactions, and (d) excluding high-energy virtual orbitals that contribute little. Including all electrons (b) defeats the purpose of active space reduction.

questions.question: What does a double excitation operator in UCCSD represent physically?

options.text: Two electrons being excited to higher energy states simultaneously
An electron moving between two orbitals twice
The interaction between two nuclei
The spin of two electrons flipping simultaneously

questions.explanation: A double excitation operator a^dag_a a^dag_b a_j a_i describes the simultaneous excitation of two electrons from occupied orbitals (i, j) to virtual orbitals (a, b). This captures important electron correlation effects that single excitations alone cannot describe.
